name: Harvey Weinstein
role_title: Co-founder and Co-chairman, Miramax Films / The Weinstein Company
charges: Forcible rape, forcible oral copulation, and sexual penetration by foreign object (Los Angeles, 2022); First-degree criminal sexual act (New York retrial, 2025)
jurisdiction: Los Angeles County Superior Court (California); Manhattan Criminal Court (New York)
case_number: BA507675 (Los Angeles); Ind. No. 02335-2018 (New York original, retried under same indictment)
outcome: California: 16 years in prison (sentenced February 23, 2023); New York 2025 retrial: convicted of one count of first-degree criminal sexual act (sentencing pending as of mid-2025). Third charge (rape) declared mistrial in June 2025; retrial scheduled April 2026. NOTE: Original 2020 New York conviction (23-year sentence) was OVERTURNED by NY Court of Appeals on April 25, 2024.
conviction_date: California conviction: December 19, 2022; sentenced February 23, 2023. New York retrial conviction: June 11, 2025.
source1_description: DOJ/NPR coverage of LA sentencing and CA conviction details
source2_description: AP News coverage of 2025 New York retrial conviction
notes: Original 2020 New York conviction (23-year sentence for criminal sexual act and third-degree rape) was overturned by the NY Court of Appeals on April 25, 2024 (People v. Weinstein, No. 24) due to improper admission of prior bad acts testimony. He was retried and reconvicted on June 11, 2025 on one count. His California 2022 conviction is currently under appeal but remains in effect. A fourth trial on the third-degree rape charge is scheduled for April 2026.

name: Bill Cosby
role_title: Actor, Comedian, Producer ('The Cosby Show')
charges: Three counts of aggravated indecent assault (drugging and sexually assaulting Andrea Constand in 2004)
jurisdiction: Montgomery County Court of Common Pleas, Pennsylvania
outcome: Convicted on all three counts (April 26, 2018). Sentenced to 3 to 10 years in state prison (September 25, 2018). Conviction VACATED by Pennsylvania Supreme Court on June 30, 2021. Released from prison after serving approximately 3 years.
source1_description: ABC News coverage of Bill Cosby conviction being overturned
source2_description: New York Times coverage of Cosby release
notes: The Pennsylvania Supreme Court overturned the conviction because a prior prosecutor (Bruce Castor) had agreed not to criminally prosecute Cosby in exchange for his deposition in a civil case. The court ruled this agreement was binding and its violation was a due process violation. Cosby was not acquitted; the case was dismissed on procedural grounds. He cannot be retried under double jeopardy principles.

name: Danny Masterson
role_title: Actor ('That '70s Show')
charges: Two counts of forcible rape (raping two women at his Hollywood Hills home in 2003)
jurisdiction: Los Angeles County Superior Court, California
outcome: Convicted on two counts of forcible rape (May 31, 2023, after retrial). Sentenced to 30 years to life in prison on September 7, 2023. Currently incarcerated at California Men's Colony in San Luis Obispo. Filed habeas corpus petition November 2025 alleging ineffective assistance of counsel.
source1_description: NBC News coverage of Danny Masterson sentencing
source2_description: New York Times coverage of Masterson sentencing
notes: First trial in 2022 ended in mistrial (jurors leaning toward acquittal). Retried in 2023 and convicted on two of three counts; third count was a mistrial. Masterson is a member of the Church of Scientology. Habeas corpus petition pending as of late 2025.

name: R. Kelly (Robert Sylvester Kelly)
role_title: R&B Singer, Music Producer
charges: Racketeering, sexual exploitation of children, forced labor, Mann Act violations (Eastern District of New York); Production of child pornography and enticement of a minor (Northern District of Illinois)
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Eastern District of New York (Brooklyn); U.S. District Court, Northern District of Illinois (Chicago)
outcome: EDNY: Convicted on all 9 counts (September 27, 2021); sentenced to 30 years in prison (June 29, 2022). NDIL: Convicted on 6 of 13 counts including 3 counts of child pornography production (September 14, 2022); sentenced to 20 years, with 1 year to run consecutively to EDNY sentence (February 23, 2023). Total effective sentence: approximately 31 years. Also fined and ordered to pay restitution.
source1_description: DOJ press release: R. Kelly convicted in Brooklyn federal court
source2_description: DOJ press release: R. Kelly sentenced to 30 years in EDNY
notes: Second Circuit Court of Appeals affirmed EDNY conviction on February 12, 2025. Seventh Circuit affirmed NDIL conviction on April 26, 2024. Kelly was also convicted in 2008 on state child pornography charges in Illinois but was acquitted at trial.

name: Keith Raniere
role_title: Founder and Leader of NXIVM (self-improvement organization/cult)
charges: Racketeering, racketeering conspiracy, sex trafficking, attempted sex trafficking, sex trafficking conspiracy, forced labor conspiracy, wire fraud conspiracy
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Eastern District of New York (Brooklyn)
outcome: Convicted on all counts by federal jury (June 19, 2019). Sentenced to 120 years in prison and fined $1,750,000 (October 27, 2020).
source1_description: DOJ press release: NXIVM leader Keith Raniere sentenced to 120 years
source2_description: BBC News coverage of Raniere sentencing
notes: NXIVM was a self-help organization that operated as a sex cult. Raniere had recruited actress Allison Mack and others to lead a secret women's group (DOS) in which members were branded and forced to engage in sexual acts. Clare Bronfman, heiress to the Seagram's liquor fortune, was also convicted in the same case.

name: Allison Mack
role_title: Actress ('Smallville'); Senior NXIVM member and DOS 'First-Line Master'
charges: Racketeering and racketeering conspiracy (admitted to state law extortion and forced labor as underlying acts)
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Eastern District of New York (Brooklyn)
outcome: Pleaded guilty on April 8, 2019. Sentenced to 3 years in prison, 3 years probation, 1,000 hours of community service, and a $20,000 fine (June 30, 2021). Released from Federal Correctional Institution Dublin, California on July 3, 2023 after serving approximately 21 months.
source1_description: NPR coverage of Allison Mack sentencing
source2_description: BBC News coverage of Allison Mack's early release from prison
notes: Mack received a significantly reduced sentence (from a potential 14-17.5 years) due to her substantial cooperation with prosecutors, providing detailed and highly corroborated information that aided in convicting Raniere.

name: Phil Spector
role_title: Record Producer, Songwriter (created 'Wall of Sound'; produced albums for The Beatles, Ramones, and others)
charges: Second-degree murder (of actress Lana Clarkson at his Alhambra, California mansion on February 3, 2003); personal use of firearm in commission of a crime
jurisdiction: Los Angeles County Superior Court, California
outcome: First trial (2007) ended in mistrial. Retried and convicted of second-degree murder plus firearm enhancement (April 13, 2009). Sentenced to 19 years to life in prison (May 29, 2009). Died in prison hospital on January 16, 2021 of COVID-19 complications.
source1_description: CNN coverage of Phil Spector sentencing
source2_description: Wikipedia: Murder of Lana Clarkson (with extensive court record citations)
notes: Conviction was upheld by the California Second District Court of Appeal in May 2011. U.S. Supreme Court denied review in February 2012. All subsequent habeas corpus petitions were denied. Spector died in prison in 2021.

name: Roman Polanski
role_title: Film Director ('Rosemary's Baby,' 'Chinatown,' 'The Pianist')
charges: Engaging in unlawful sexual intercourse with a minor (Samantha Gailey, age 13); accepted as part of plea bargain in which 5 more serious charges were dismissed
jurisdiction: Los Angeles County Superior Court, California
outcome: Pleaded guilty on August 8, 1977. Ordered to serve 90-day psychiatric evaluation at Chino State Prison; released after 42 days. Fled to France on January 31, 1978 before formal sentencing hearing, becoming a fugitive. Has not been formally sentenced to this day.
source1_description: Wikipedia: Roman Polanski sexual abuse case
source2_description: Courthouse News: Polanski Case - What We Know After 40 Years
notes: Polanski entered a guilty plea and is technically a convicted felon, but fled before sentencing and has remained a fugitive from U.S. justice since 1978. He has lived primarily in France (a French citizen, France does not extradite its citizens to the U.S.). He was arrested in Switzerland in 2009 but extradition was denied. The guilty plea and conviction remain on the books; there has been no overturning.

name: Kevin Spacey
role_title: Actor (House of Cards, American Beauty), Artistic Director of the Old Vic Theatre
charges: No criminal conviction. Tried in London in 2023 on 9 counts of sexual assault and 1 count of causing a person to engage in sexual activity without consent.
jurisdiction: Southwark Crown Court, London, UK
outcome: ACQUITTED on all charges (July 26, 2023). U.S. civil lawsuit dismissed by jury in 2022. In March 2026, Spacey settled UK civil lawsuits filed by accusers before a scheduled civil trial.
source1_description: Variety coverage of Kevin Spacey's 2026 UK civil settlement
source2_description: Reuters coverage of Spacey UK settlement
notes: EXCLUDED from main list - Kevin Spacey was ACQUITTED, not convicted. Included here for reference as he was specifically requested for verification. He was acquitted of all criminal charges in London in 2023, and a prior U.S. civil case was dismissed. He settled UK civil lawsuits in March 2026 but denied wrongdoing.

name: Victor Salva
role_title: Film Director, Writer ('Clownhouse,' 'Jeepers Creepers' franchise)
charges: Lewd and lascivious conduct; oral copulation with a person under 14; three counts of procuring a child for pornography (involving 12-year-old actor Nathan Forrest Winters on the set of 'Clownhouse')
jurisdiction: Contra Costa County Superior Court, California
outcome: Pleaded guilty to five felony counts (April 1988). Sentenced to 3 years in state prison. Served 15 months before parole. Completed parole in 1992. Registered as a lifetime sex offender.
source1_description: Wikipedia: Victor Salva (with court record citations)
source2_description: IndieWire: 'Jeepers Creepers 3' Director Victor Salva's Criminal History
notes: Salva went on to direct major Hollywood films including 'Powder' (1995, Disney) and the 'Jeepers Creepers' franchise after completing his sentence. His victim Nathan Forrest Winters publicly protested the release of 'Powder.' Florida sex offender registry still lists Salva with an adjudication date of April 11, 1988.

name: Mark Salling
role_title: Actor ('Glee,' played Noah 'Puck' Puckerman)
charges: Possession of child pornography (federal)
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Central District of California
outcome: Pleaded guilty to one count of possession of child pornography (September 30, 2017; formalized December 18, 2017). Sentencing set for March 7, 2018. Salling died by suicide on January 30, 2018, before sentencing. All criminal penalties were vacated upon his death.
source1_description: DOJ press release: Actor Mark Wayne Salling indicted for child pornography
source2_description: ABC News coverage of Salling's guilty plea
notes: Salling's guilty plea constitutes a conviction, though sentencing never occurred due to his death by suicide. His plea agreement included terms of 4-7 years in prison and $50,000 minimum restitution per victim. He had approximately 50,000+ images and 600 videos of child pornography. Because death preceded sentencing, the conviction was effectively vacated.
status: CONVICTED (guilty plea; deceased before sentencing — penalties vacated)

name: Michael Jace
role_title: Actor (Officer Julien Lowe on 'The Shield')
charges: Second-degree murder (fatally shot his wife April Jace at their home in Los Angeles on May 19, 2014, in front of their two young sons)
jurisdiction: Los Angeles County Superior Court, California
outcome: Convicted by jury of second-degree murder and firearm use enhancement (May 31, 2016). Sentenced to 40 years to life in prison (15 years to life for murder + 25 years to life for gun enhancement) on June 10, 2016. Currently incarcerated at Corcoran State Prison.
source1_description: ABC News coverage of Michael Jace's sentencing
source2_description: CNN coverage of Michael Jace's 40-year sentence
notes: Jace called 911 himself after shooting his wife. He told detectives he had retrieved the gun to kill himself but shot his wife instead. He had significant financial problems at the time of the crime.

name: Ryan Grantham
role_title: Actor ('Riverdale,' 'Diary of a Wimpy Kid,' 'Supernatural')
charges: Second-degree murder (killed his mother Barbara Waite by shooting her in the back of the head on March 31, 2020, in Squamish, British Columbia)
jurisdiction: British Columbia Supreme Court, Vancouver, Canada
outcome: Pleaded guilty to second-degree murder (originally charged with first-degree). Sentenced to life in prison with parole ineligibility of 14 years (September 21, 2022).
source1_description: CBC coverage of Ryan Grantham's life sentence
source2_description: BBC News: Riverdale actor Ryan Grantham gets life in prison
notes: After murdering his mother, Grantham filmed a GoPro video confessing to the killing. He had also planned to travel to Ottawa to assassinate Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau but did not carry it out. He will not be eligible for parole until 2034.

name: Kaalan 'KR' Walker
role_title: Actor ('Superfly,' 'To All the Boys I've Loved Before') and Rapper
charges: Three counts of forcible rape, one count of assault with intent to commit oral copulation, two counts of statutory rape (sexually assaulting multiple aspiring models and women he lured through fake professional promises on social media, 2013–2018)
jurisdiction: Los Angeles County Superior Court, California
outcome: Convicted on 8 counts (including 3 forcible rapes) by jury (April 28, 2022). Sentenced to 50 years to life in prison (October 17, 2022).
source1_description: NBC News coverage of Kaalan Walker's sentencing
source2_description: IMDb News: 'Superfly' Actor Kaalan Walker Gets 50-Year Sentence
notes: Walker used his entertainment industry connections to lure victims under false pretenses of professional opportunities before assaulting them. Prosecutors said he committed the crimes against aspiring models and actresses across Los Angeles.

name: Corey Miller (C-Murder)
role_title: Rapper (No Limit Records); brother of Master P (Percy Miller)
charges: Second-degree murder (fatally shot 16-year-old fan Steven Thomas at the Platinum Club nightclub in Harvey, Louisiana on January 12, 2002)
jurisdiction: 24th Judicial District Court, Jefferson Parish, Louisiana
outcome: Convicted of second-degree murder (August 11, 2009, after retrial). Sentenced to mandatory life imprisonment (August 14, 2009). Serving his sentence at Louisiana State Penitentiary (Angola). All appeals exhausted; Louisiana Supreme Court denied appeal unanimously on February 3, 2026.
source1_description: Hollywood Reporter: C-Murder convicted of killing fan
source2_description: Complex: C-Murder's Murder Conviction Appeal Denied for a Third Time (2026)
notes: Miller's first conviction in 2003 was overturned on appeal due to jury issues. He was retried and convicted again in 2009. He has consistently maintained his innocence. Two witnesses recanted in 2018. The Louisiana Supreme Court unanimously denied his third appeal on February 3, 2026, exhausting state remedies.

name: Ghislaine Maxwell
role_title: Socialite; close associate of Jeffrey Epstein; active in entertainment and social circles
charges: Conspiracy to entice minors to travel to engage in illegal sex acts; conspiracy to transport minors to participate in illegal sex acts; transporting a minor to participate in illegal sex acts; sex trafficking conspiracy; sex trafficking of a minor
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Southern District of New York
outcome: Convicted on 5 of 6 counts by jury (December 29, 2021). Sentenced to 20 years in prison and fined $750,000 (June 28, 2022). Second Circuit Court of Appeals affirmed conviction on September 17, 2024.
source1_description: DOJ press release: Ghislaine Maxwell sentenced to 20 years
source2_description: Justia: Second Circuit Court of Appeals affirms Maxwell conviction (Sept 2024)
notes: Maxwell recruited and groomed young girls for Jeffrey Epstein over a decade (approximately 1994–2004). While not exclusively an entertainment figure, Maxwell was a prominent fixture in entertainment, media, and social circles. Epstein died in prison in 2019 before trial.

name: Marc Collins-Rector
role_title: Co-founder and Chairman, Digital Entertainment Network (DEN) — early internet entertainment company
charges: Eight counts of child enticement (transporting five minors across state lines for sexual purposes, 1995–1999)
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, District of New Jersey; related charges in U.S. District Court, Central District of California
outcome: Pleaded guilty to eight counts of child enticement (June 10, 2004). Sentenced to time served (approximately 27-33 months, served partly in Spain while fighting extradition) plus lifetime sex offender registration.
source1_description: Los Angeles Times: Former Net Exec Enters Guilty Plea (2004)
source2_description: Wikipedia: Marc Collins-Rector
notes: Collins-Rector co-founded DEN with Brock Pierce (later cryptocurrency entrepreneur) and Chad Shackley. DEN raised significant venture capital before collapsing in 1999 amid allegations of sexual abuse. Collins-Rector fled to Spain before returning to face charges. DEN was the subject of the 2014 documentary 'An Open Secret.'

name: Lauryn Hill
role_title: Singer-Songwriter, Rapper (The Fugees; Grammy-winning solo artist)
charges: Three misdemeanor counts of failure to file federal income tax returns (for tax years 2005, 2006, and 2007; approximately $1.8 million in unreported income)
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, District of New Jersey
outcome: Pleaded guilty to all three counts (June 2012). Sentenced to 3 months in prison, 3 months home confinement, and 9 months supervised release (May 6, 2013, by Magistrate Judge Madeline Cox Arleo). Paid all back taxes plus additional amounts (approximately $970,000 total).
source1_description: BBC News: Lauryn Hill jailed for tax evasion
source2_description: WAMC: Singer Lauryn Hill Sentenced to Three Months for Tax Evasion
notes: Hill stated that she withheld taxes during a period of conflict and anti-establishment feelings. She paid all back taxes and additional amounts before sentencing, which contributed to receiving the lighter 3-month sentence rather than the prosecutors' recommended 36-month maximum.

name: Todd Chrisley
role_title: Reality TV personality ('Chrisley Knows Best'), real estate developer
charges: Conspiracy to commit bank fraud, bank fraud, wire fraud, conspiracy to defraud the United States (tax evasion), tax evasion (defrauded community banks of over $36 million; evaded taxes while earning millions from TV show)
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Northern District of Georgia (Atlanta)
outcome: Convicted on all counts by jury (June 7, 2022). Sentenced to 12 years in prison and ordered to pay $17.8 million in restitution (November 21, 2022). Began sentence January 17, 2023. Conviction upheld by 11th Circuit on June 21, 2024. PARDONED by President Trump on May 27, 2025, and released from prison.
source1_description: DOJ press release: Television personalities sentenced for fraud and tax evasion
source2_description: AP News: Trump pardons Chrisley reality TV stars
notes: Todd and Julie Chrisley were both convicted and sentenced in the same proceeding. Julie Chrisley received 7 years. Both were pardoned by President Trump on May 27, 2025, after serving over 2 years in prison. A presidential pardon does not erase the conviction but does forgive the criminal offense and restores certain civil rights.
status: CONVICTED (subsequently pardoned by President Trump, May 2025)

name: Julie Chrisley
role_title: Reality TV personality ('Chrisley Knows Best')
charges: Conspiracy to commit bank fraud, bank fraud, wire fraud, conspiracy to defraud the United States (tax evasion), obstruction of justice
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Northern District of Georgia (Atlanta)
outcome: Convicted on all counts by jury (June 7, 2022). Sentenced to 7 years in prison and ordered to pay restitution (November 21, 2022). Began sentence January 17, 2023. Sentence upheld on resentencing September 25, 2024. PARDONED by President Trump on May 27, 2025.
source1_description: DOJ press release: Television personalities sentenced for fraud and tax evasion
source2_description: People: Todd and Julie Chrisley Pardon Details
notes: See Todd Chrisley entry for background on the scheme. Julie's sentence was partially challenged on appeal; the 11th Circuit vacated and remanded for resentencing on Julie's case due to guideline miscalculations, but on remand the court reimposed the original 7-year sentence. Both were pardoned by President Trump in May 2025.
status: CONVICTED (subsequently pardoned by President Trump, May 2025)

name: J. Mitchell Lowe
role_title: CEO of MoviePass; co-founder of Netflix
charges: Conspiracy to commit securities fraud (defrauding investors of MoviePass parent company Helios & Matheson Analytics by making materially false and misleading statements about the company's business model and operations)
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Southern District of Florida
outcome: Pleaded guilty to one count of conspiracy to commit securities fraud (September 16, 2024). Sentencing pending as of early 2025 (status conference scheduled for mid-2025). Faces a maximum of 5 years in federal prison.
source1_description: DOJ case page: United States v. Theodore Farnsworth and J. Mitchell Lowe
source2_description: Variety: Ex-MoviePass CEO Pleads Guilty to Securities Fraud
notes: Lowe and co-defendant Theodore Farnsworth (HMNY Chairman and CEO) conspired to defraud investors by falsely claiming MoviePass's $9.95 unlimited plan was tested, sustainable, and would be profitable. They also secretly 'throttled' subscribers to prevent them from using the service while publicly denying this. Lowe agreed to cooperate with the government.

name: Theodore Farnsworth
role_title: Chairman and CEO, Helios & Matheson Analytics Inc. (parent company of MoviePass)
charges: Securities fraud and conspiracy to commit securities fraud (two cases: MoviePass/HMNY and Vinco Ventures)
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Southern District of Florida (criminal); U.S. District Court, Southern District of New York (SEC civil)
outcome: Pleaded guilty to one count of securities fraud and one count of conspiracy to commit securities fraud (January 7, 2025). Sentencing pending (status conference July 2025). Faces maximum of 20 years on securities fraud count and 5 years on conspiracy count. Also subject to SEC civil injunctions and officer/director bar.
source1_description: DOJ case page: United States v. Theodore Farnsworth and J. Mitchell Lowe
source2_description: New York Times: Former MoviePass Executive Pleads Guilty to Fraud
notes: Farnsworth was originally indicted in November 2022 on four counts. He pleaded guilty to two counts in January 2025. In addition to the criminal case, he also faces SEC enforcement actions related to both the MoviePass/HMNY fraud and a separate scheme involving Vinco Ventures, Inc., where he allegedly misappropriated millions for personal use while inflating the stock price.

name: Khalid Itum
role_title: Executive Vice President of MoviePass / Helios & Matheson Analytics Inc.
charges: Two counts of wire fraud (embezzling approximately $260,000 from MoviePass parent company Helios & Matheson to repay personal debts from a Coachella festival party)
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Central District of California
outcome: Convicted on both counts of wire fraud by jury (March 20, 2025). Acquitted of money laundering charges. Sentencing date TBD.
source1_description: DOJ press release: Former MoviePass Executive arrested for embezzlement
source2_description: IMDb News: Former MoviePass Executive Convicted of Embezzlement
notes: Itum filed sham invoices through his company Kaleidoscope Productions LLC to have Helios & Matheson cover his personal debts from organizing a Coachella event. The scheme involved approximately $260,000 paid to his company for services that were never rendered for MoviePass.

name: Joe Francis
role_title: Founder and CEO, Girls Gone Wild entertainment brand
charges: Multiple convictions over multiple cases: (1) Three counts of false imprisonment, one count of assault causing great bodily injury, one count of dissuading a witness (2011 assault case); (2) No-contest plea to child abuse and prostitution charges (Panama City, FL filming of underage girls); (3) Prior record-keeping violations (2006 federal guilty plea)
jurisdiction: Los Angeles County Superior Court (assault/false imprisonment); Leon County, Florida (child abuse/prostitution); U.S. District Court, Central District of California (2006 federal case)
outcome: (1) 2013 assault conviction: 270 days county jail, 3 years probation, anger management (sentenced August 27, 2013). (2) 2015 child abuse/prostitution no-contest plea: 336 days jail (credited for time served, no additional time), 6 months probation, 3-year filming ban in Panama City area. (3) 2006 federal: $500,000 fine + $1.6 million in company fines.
conviction_date: May 6, 2013 (assault verdict); 2015 (child abuse/prostitution no-contest plea); September 26, 2006 (federal guilty plea)
source1_description: DOJ press release: Joseph Francis pleads guilty (2006 federal case)
source2_description: Los Angeles Times: Girls Gone Wild creator Joe Francis sentenced to jail for assault
notes: Francis has an extensive criminal history. In the 2006 federal case, he pled guilty to failure to maintain age verification records for performers in sexually explicit films. In 2013, he was convicted of assaulting three women at his L.A. mansion. In 2015, he pled no contest to child abuse and prostitution related to the filming of underage girls during spring break.

name: Harvey Weinstein (California — New York 2020 original OVERTURNED)
role_title: Co-founder and Co-chairman, Miramax Films / The Weinstein Company
charges: New York 2020 (OVERTURNED): First-degree criminal sexual act and third-degree rape
jurisdiction: New York Supreme Court, Manhattan
outcome: OVERTURNED. Original conviction on February 24, 2020; sentenced to 23 years. NY Court of Appeals overturned conviction 4-3 on April 25, 2024 (People v. Weinstein, No. 24) due to improper admission of uncharged prior bad acts testimony. New trial ordered and conducted in 2025.
source1_description: Justia: People v. Weinstein, NY Court of Appeals 2024 decision overturning conviction
source2_description: NPR: Weinstein's 2020 sex crime conviction was overturned
notes: Listed separately to document the overturned 2020 NY conviction. The same conduct was retried in 2025, resulting in a new conviction on one count (see main Weinstein entry above).

name: Daystar Shemuel Shua Peterson (Tory Lanez)
role_title: Canadian Rapper / Singer
charges: Assault with a semiautomatic firearm, carrying a loaded, unregistered firearm in a vehicle, discharging a firearm with gross negligence (3 felony counts)
jurisdiction: Los Angeles County Superior Court (California State)
outcome: Convicted December 23, 2022 on all 3 counts. Sentenced August 8, 2023 to 10 years in state prison by Judge David Herriford. Denied bail pending appeal; currently incarcerated.
source1_description: Los Angeles Times — Tory Lanez sentenced to 10 years for shooting Megan Thee Stallion
source2_description: NPR — Tory Lanez gets 10 years in prison for shooting Megan Thee Stallion
notes: Lanez shot rapper Megan Thee Stallion (Megan Pete) in both feet following an argument after a party in Hollywood Hills on July 12, 2020. High-profile case involving two prominent hip-hop artists. Lanez's defense claimed Megan's former friend Kelsey Harris actually fired the shots, but jury convicted Lanez.

name: Daniel Hernandez (Tekashi 6ix9ine / 6ix9ine)
role_title: Rapper
charges: Racketeering conspiracy, firearms offenses, drug trafficking conspiracy (related to Nine Trey Gangsta Bloods gang activity)
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Southern District of New York (Federal)
outcome: Arrested November 2018. Pleaded guilty February 2019 to 9 federal counts. Cooperated extensively with prosecutors, testifying against former associates. Sentenced December 18, 2019 to 24 months in prison (with time served ~13 months), 5 years supervised release. Released early April 2020 due to COVID-19 and asthma (served remainder under home confinement).
source1_description: DOJ press release — Rapper Tekashi 6ix9ine Sentenced
source2_description: NPR — Tekashi 6ix9ine Released Early From Prison Due To Coronavirus
notes: 6ix9ine was member of Nine Trey Gangsta Bloods and participated in multiple violent crimes including armed robbery and shootings. Became government cooperator, testifying against gang members. Received unusually lenient sentence due to cooperation. Faced widespread criticism in hip-hop community for 'snitching.' Released 2020.

name: Marion Hugh Knight Jr. (Suge Knight)
role_title: Co-founder and CEO, Death Row Records
charges: Voluntary manslaughter (killing Terry Carter), plus separate robbery conviction
jurisdiction: Los Angeles County Superior Court (California State)
outcome: Charged with murder and attempted murder for 2015 hit-and-run killing. Pleaded no contest to voluntary manslaughter September 20, 2018. Sentenced October 4, 2018 to 28 years in state prison by Judge Ronald Coen. Currently incarcerated; earliest possible release 2037.
source1_description: Los Angeles Times — Suge Knight sentenced to 28 years in prison for fatal hit-and-run
source2_description: NPR — Death Row Records Co-Founder Suge Knight Gets 28 Years For Fatal Hit-And-Run
notes: Knight ran over two men with his truck outside a Compton burger stand in January 2015, killing Terry Carter (55) and injuring Cle 'Bone' Sloan. Knight claimed self-defense. Legendary (and notorious) figure in 1990s gangsta rap; Death Row Records released albums by Tupac, Snoop Dogg, Dr. Dre.

name: Antron Singleton (Big Lurch)
role_title: Rapper
charges: Murder, aggravated mayhem (killed and partially cannibalized victim while under influence of PCP)
jurisdiction: Los Angeles County Superior Court (California State)
outcome: Convicted 2003. Sentenced to life in prison without possibility of parole. Currently incarcerated at California State Prison, Lancaster.
source1_description: Los Angeles Times — Rapper convicted of killing, mutilating woman
source2_description: Vice — The Rapper Who Went to Prison for Cannibalism
notes: In April 2002, while high on PCP, Big Lurch killed his roommate Tynisha Ysais (21) and mutilated her body, tearing open her chest and eating parts of her lungs. Police found him naked and covered in blood, screaming at the sky. Medical examination found human flesh in his stomach. One of the most horrific crimes in hip-hop history.

name: Taymor Travon McIntyre (Tay-K)
role_title: Rapper
charges: Murder, aggravated robbery (killed Ethan Walker, 21, during home invasion)
jurisdiction: Tarrant County Criminal District Court, Texas (State)
outcome: Convicted July 19, 2019 of murder and 3 counts of aggravated robbery. Sentenced July 23, 2019 to 55 years in prison for murder plus 2 concurrent 30-year sentences for robbery. Also separately convicted in 2019 for involvement in second murder (60 years, to run concurrent). Earliest possible release in his 70s.
source1_description: NPR — Rapper Tay-K Sentenced To 55 Years For Murder
source2_description: Dallas Morning News — Tay-K sentenced to 55 years for murder
notes: Tay-K participated in 2016 home invasion robbery that resulted in murder of Ethan Walker. While on house arrest awaiting trial in 2017, he cut off ankle monitor and fled, going on the run. During this time, he recorded and released viral hit 'The Race' which peaked at #44 on Billboard Hot 100. Arrested in New Jersey June 2017. Also connected to second murder committed while on the run.

name: Johnathan Jamall Porter (Blueface)
role_title: Rapper
charges: Attempted murder with a deadly weapon, discharging a firearm at or into an occupied structure (2 felony counts)
jurisdiction: Clark County District Court, Nevada (State)
outcome: Arrested November 2022. Pleaded guilty July 2024 as part of plea deal. Sentenced October 3, 2024 to suspended sentence with probation. Credit for ~1 year time served. Released 2024 on probation.
source1_description: Las Vegas Review-Journal — Blueface sentenced in Las Vegas shooting case
source2_description: XXL — Blueface Sentenced in Las Vegas Shooting Case
notes: Blueface shot at occupied vehicle outside Las Vegas strip club in October 2022. Initially faced up to 50 years. Pleaded guilty to reduced charges. Received probation and credit for time served (~1 year), avoiding lengthy prison sentence.

name: Dwayne Michael Carter Jr. (Lil Wayne)
role_title: Rapper
charges: Possession of a firearm and ammunition by a convicted felon (federal)
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Southern District of Florida (Federal)
outcome: Arrested December 2019. Pleaded guilty December 11, 2020 to one count of possession of firearm by convicted felon. Faced up to 10 years in prison. Before sentencing, PARDONED by President Trump on January 20, 2021 (Trump's last day in office), nullifying the conviction.
conviction_date: December 11, 2020 (guilty plea); PARDONED January 20, 2021 before sentencing
source1_description: DOJ press release — Dwayne Carter, aka Lil Wayne, Pleads Guilty to Possession of a Firearm and Ammunition
source2_description: NPR — Trump Pardons Lil Wayne, Kodak Black In Final Hours
notes: Federal agents found loaded gold-plated .45 caliber handgun in Lil Wayne's luggage during search of his private jet in Miami in December 2019. As a convicted felon (2007 gun conviction), he was prohibited from possessing firearms. Pleaded guilty but was pardoned by Trump before sentencing, nullifying conviction.

name: Aaron Josef Hernandez
role_title: NFL Tight End (New England Patriots)
charges: First-degree murder (killing Odin Lloyd), unlawful possession of a firearm, unlawful possession of ammunition (Massachusetts state charges)
jurisdiction: Bristol County Superior Court, Massachusetts (State)
outcome: Convicted April 15, 2015 of first-degree murder and weapons charges. Sentenced to life in prison without possibility of parole. Committed suicide in prison April 19, 2017. Under Massachusetts law (abatement doctrine), conviction was posthumously vacated May 9, 2017 because he died before his appeals were exhausted. However, he was separately tried and acquitted of double murder in 2017 (before his death).
conviction_date: April 15, 2015 (convicted); CONVICTION VACATED May 9, 2017 (posthumously due to suicide before appeals exhausted)
source1_description: NPR — Aaron Hernandez's Murder Conviction Erased After Suicide
source2_description: ESPN — Timeline of Aaron Hernandez's murder case
notes: Hernandez murdered semi-pro football player Odin Lloyd on June 17, 2013. Convicted 2015 and sentenced to life. While in prison, was tried for separate 2012 double murder (Daniel de Abreu and Safiro Furtado) but was acquitted April 14, 2017. Five days later, committed suicide in prison. Murder conviction vacated under abatement doctrine (like Ken Lay). Fall from NFL star to convicted murderer shocked sports world.

name: Michael Gerard Tyson (Mike Tyson)
role_title: Professional Boxer / Former Heavyweight Champion
charges: Rape, two counts of criminal deviate conduct (Indiana state charges)
jurisdiction: Marion County Superior Court, Indiana (State)
outcome: Convicted February 10, 1992 on all three counts. Sentenced March 26, 1992 to 6 years in Indiana state prison (10 years, with 4 suspended). Served 3 years; released March 25, 1995 after serving approximately half his sentence.
source1_description: New York Times — Tyson Gets 6-Year Prison Term For Rape Conviction
source2_description: Washington Post — Mike Tyson: Rape conviction and prison time
notes: Tyson was convicted of raping 18-year-old Miss Black America contestant Desiree Washington in an Indianapolis hotel room in July 1991. At the time, Tyson was undisputed heavyweight boxing champion. He maintained his innocence throughout. Released 1995 and returned to boxing. Conviction remains highly controversial with defenders claiming he was falsely accused.

name: Michael Dwayne Vick (Michael Vick)
role_title: NFL Quarterback (Atlanta Falcons)
charges: Conspiracy to travel in interstate commerce in aid of unlawful activities and to sponsor a dog in an animal fighting venture (federal dogfighting charges)
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Eastern District of Virginia (Federal)
outcome: Indicted July 2007. Pleaded guilty August 27, 2007. Sentenced December 10, 2007 by Judge Henry Hudson to 23 months in federal prison. Served 21 months (released May 2009 to home confinement; fully released July 2009). Also served 3 years federal probation. Declared bankruptcy.
source1_description: DOJ press release — Michael Vick Sentenced on Dogfighting Conspiracy Charge
source2_description: NPR — Michael Vick Sentenced To 23 Months For Dogfighting
notes: Vick ran 'Bad Newz Kennels' dogfighting operation on Virginia property 2001-2007. Operation included interstate dogfighting, gambling, and killing underperforming dogs through hanging, drowning, electrocution. Vick personally participated in killing dogs. NFL suspended him indefinitely; Falcons terminated his contract. After release, remarkably resurrected NFL career with Philadelphia Eagles and other teams. Became animal rights advocate.

name: Henry Ruggs III
role_title: NFL Wide Receiver (Las Vegas Raiders)
charges: DUI resulting in death, reckless driving resulting in death (Nevada state charges)
jurisdiction: Clark County District Court, Nevada (State)
outcome: Arrested November 2, 2021. Pleaded guilty May 10, 2023 to DUI resulting in death and vehicular manslaughter. Sentenced August 9, 2023 to 3-10 years in Nevada state prison. Currently incarcerated; eligible for parole after 3 years (2026).
source1_description: Las Vegas Review-Journal — Henry Ruggs sentenced to at least 3 years in prison
source2_description: NPR — Ex-NFL player Henry Ruggs III sentenced to at least 3 years for fatal DUI crash
notes: Ruggs was driving 156 mph with blood alcohol level twice legal limit when his Corvette slammed into Toyota RAV4 driven by Tina Tintor (23) and her dog on November 2, 2021 in Las Vegas. Tintor and her dog burned to death in the resulting fire. Ruggs had just signed $16 million rookie contract with Raiders as 12th overall pick in 2020 NFL Draft. Raiders immediately released him. Faced up to 50 years but pleaded guilty to reduced charges.

name: Brian Peck
role_title: Dialogue coach and actor; worked on Nickelodeon (The Amanda Show, All That) and Disney Channel (The Suite Life of Zack & Cody)
charges: Lewd act upon a child (14–15 years of age), oral copulation with a minor under 16 — sexually abused actor Drake Bell over a six-month period in 2001 when Bell was 15 years old (registered sex offender for life)
jurisdiction: California state court, Los Angeles
outcome: 16 months; lifetime sex offender registration
conviction_date: 2004 (arrested Aug. 2003; pleaded no contest May; sentenced October)
source1_description: Los Angeles Times coverage of Peck conviction
source2_description: Drake Bell disclosure in 'Quiet on Set' documentary

name: Jeffrey Jones
role_title: Actor; Amadeus (1984), Ferris Bueller's Day Off (1986) as Principal Rooney, Beetlejuice (1988)
charges: Solicitation of a minor to pose for nude photographs, possession of child pornography — paid a 14-year-old boy to pose for nude photos at his home 1999–2001; 2010 felony conviction for failing to register as sex offender
jurisdiction: California state court, Los Angeles
outcome: 5 years probation; lifetime sex offender registration; 2010 felony: 250 hours community service + 3 years probation
source1_description: Los Angeles Times coverage of Jones conviction
source2_description: 2010 felony conviction for failing to register

name: Jerry Harris
role_title: Cheerleader and media personality; starred in 2020 Netflix docuseries Cheer
charges: Receiving child pornography, traveling with intent to engage in illegal sexual conduct with a minor — coerced teenage boys to send obscene photos/videos and solicited sex from minors at cheerleading competitions
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, N.D. Illinois (federal)
outcome: 12 years; 8 years court-supervised release
conviction_date: 2022 (arrested Sep. 2020; pleaded guilty Feb.; sentenced Jul. 6)
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Harris sentencing
source2_description: Chicago Tribune coverage

name: Joe Exotic (Joseph Maldonado-Passage)
role_title: Zoo owner, exotic animal trainer, country music performer; subject of Netflix docuseries Tiger King: Murder, Mayhem and Madness (2020)
charges: Two counts murder-for-hire (to kill Big Cat Rescue founder Carole Baskin); 8 counts violating the Lacey Act; 9 counts violating the Endangered Species Act — 17 federal charges total
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, W.D. Oklahoma (federal)
outcome: 21 years in federal prison
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Joe Exotic conviction
source2_description: Oklahoma news coverage

name: Jen Shah (Jennifer Shah)
role_title: Reality television personality; cast member of The Real Housewives of Salt Lake City (Bravo)
charges: Conspiracy to commit wire fraud in connection with telemarketing — operated a nationwide fraud scheme for approximately a decade (2012–2021) targeting thousands of victims, many elderly, offering fake 'business services'
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, S.D. New York (federal)
outcome: 78 months / 6.5 years; $6.6 million restitution
conviction_date: 2023 (arrested Mar. 2021; pleaded guilty Jul. 2022; sentenced Jan. 6)
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Jen Shah sentencing
source2_description: New York Times coverage

name: Billy McFarland
role_title: Entrepreneur and co-founder of Fyre Festival; media personality
charges: Wire fraud (2 counts) — defrauded over 80 investors in Fyre Media Inc. and Fyre Festival LLC of over $26 million by misrepresenting the company's finances and the festival's viability
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, S.D. New York (federal)
outcome: 6 years; $26+ million restitution
conviction_date: 2018 (arrested Jun. 2017; pleaded guilty Mar. 6; sentenced Oct. 11)
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on McFarland sentencing
source2_description: New York Times coverage

name: Don King
role_title: Boxing promoter; promoted fights involving Muhammad Ali, Joe Frazier, George Foreman, Mike Tyson, and others
charges: Second-degree murder — beat employee Sam Garrett to death on April 20, 1967 in Cleveland, Ohio, because Garrett owed him $600; conviction later reduced to voluntary manslaughter on appeal
jurisdiction: Ohio state court, Cuyahoga County
outcome: Manslaughter; 1–20 years; served 3 years 11 months; pardoned by Ohio Governor Jim Rhodes 1983
source1_description: Cleveland Plain Dealer coverage of Don King case
source2_description: Boxing history documentation

name: James Brown
role_title: Musician, 'Godfather of Soul'; recording artist known for I Got You (I Feel Good), Papa's Got a Brand New Bag, Living in America, and dozens of other hits
charges: Aggravated assault (2 counts), unlawful possession of weapons, failing to stop for police — led police on a multi-state car chase across South Carolina and Georgia after erratic behavior and drug use (PCP)
jurisdiction: South Carolina state court
outcome: 6.5 years; served ~26 months before parole Feb. 27, 1991; pardoned by South Carolina May 20, 2003
source1_description: South Carolina court documentation
source2_description: New York Times coverage

name: Anna Delvey (Anna Sorokin)
role_title: Con artist and celebrity socialite; posed as a wealthy German heiress ('Anna Delvey') to defraud New York's social elite, banks, and hotels; subject of Netflix series Inventing Anna (2022)
charges: Grand larceny in the second degree (3 counts), attempted grand larceny in the first degree, theft of services (4 counts) — defrauded financial institutions, hotels, and acquaintances by misrepresenting a multi-million-euro trust fund and forging bank documents; stole approximately $275,000
jurisdiction: New York state court, Manhattan
outcome: 4–12 years; $24,000 fine; $199,000 restitution; released on parole Feb. 2021
source1_description: Manhattan District Attorney press release
source2_description: New York Times coverage

name: Sean "Diddy" Combs
role_title: Founder, Bad Boy Records; Music Producer, Artist, Entrepreneur
charges: Sex trafficking by force, fraud, and coercion; racketeering conspiracy; transportation to engage in prostitution
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Southern District of New York
outcome: Awaiting trial (detained without bail since September 2024); additional superseding indictment filed May 2025 adding new counts
conviction_date: Awaiting trial — indicted September 2024, superseding indictment May 2025
source1_description: DOJ SDNY indictment — United States v. Sean Combs
source2_description: Reuters coverage of Combs arrest and charges
notes: Combs was arrested September 16, 2024 and has been held in federal detention. The superseding indictment filed May 2025 added new charges and named additional alleged victims. Trial date set for May 2025.

name: Lori Loughlin
role_title: Actress ('Full House', 'When Calls the Heart')
charges: Conspiracy to commit wire and mail fraud; honest services fraud — paid $500,000 bribe to secure daughters' admission to USC as fake crew recruits
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, District of Massachusetts
outcome: 2 months in federal prison, $150,000 fine, 2 years supervised probation, 100 hours community service
source1_description: DOJ press release — Lori Loughlin sentenced to 2 months in prison
source2_description: NPR coverage of Loughlin sentencing

name: Mossimo Giannulli
role_title: Fashion Designer (Mossimo brand)
charges: Conspiracy to commit wire and mail fraud; honest services fraud — paid $500,000 bribe to secure daughters' admission to USC as fake crew recruits
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, District of Massachusetts
outcome: 5 months in federal prison, $250,000 fine, 2 years supervised probation, 250 hours community service
source1_description: DOJ press release — Mossimo Giannulli sentenced to 5 months in prison
source2_description: AP News — Lori Loughlin's husband sentenced to 5 months in prison

name: Felicity Huffman
role_title: Actress ('Desperate Housewives')
charges: Conspiracy to commit mail fraud and honest services mail fraud — paid $15,000 to have SAT proctor correct daughter's test answers
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, District of Massachusetts
outcome: 14 days in federal prison, $30,000 fine, 1 year supervised probation, 250 hours community service
source1_description: DOJ press release — Felicity Huffman sentenced for college admission fraud
source2_description: ABC News — Felicity Huffman sentenced to 14 days in prison

name: Rick Singer
role_title: College Admissions Consultant, Founder, Edge College & Career Network (The Key)
charges: Racketeering conspiracy, money laundering conspiracy, conspiracy to defraud the United States, obstruction of justice — operated $25M college admissions bribery scheme
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, District of Massachusetts
outcome: 3.5 years in federal prison; sentenced January 4, 2023
source1_description: DOJ press release — Rick Singer sentenced to 3.5 years
source2_description: NPR — 'Varsity Blues' mastermind Rick Singer sentenced

name: Austin Jones
role_title: YouTube Singer/Musician (1.1M subscribers)
charges: Two counts of production of child pornography — solicited sexually explicit videos from underage female fans aged 14–15
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Northern District of Illinois
outcome: 10 years in federal prison; sentenced June 4, 2019
source1_description: DOJ press release — Austin Jones sentenced to 10 years for child pornography
source2_description: NBC News — YouTube star Austin Jones sentenced to 10 years

name: Young Thug (Jeffery Lamar Williams)
role_title: Rapper, Co-founder YSL Records
charges: RICO conspiracy; participation in criminal street gang (Young Slime Life/YSL); drug trafficking; murder; assault
jurisdiction: Fulton County Superior Court, Georgia (state)
outcome: Pleaded guilty August 2024 to RICO conspiracy and one count of gang activity; sentenced to 15 years with 7 years to serve, remainder on probation
source1_description: AP News — Young Thug pleads guilty in Atlanta gang trial
source2_description: NPR — Young Thug pleads guilty, avoids further trial

name: Kay Flock (Kevin Perez)
role_title: Rapper (drill music, New York)
charges: Murder in the first degree (shooting death of Rodman Hilliman, December 2021); gang-related charges
jurisdiction: New York Supreme Court, Bronx County (state); U.S. District Court, S.D. New York (federal)
outcome: Pleaded guilty to first-degree murder in state court; sentenced to 15 years to life, February 2025

source2_description: Complex — Kay Flock sentenced for murder of Bronx man

name: NBA YoungBoy (Kentrell Gaulden)
role_title: Rapper
charges: Possession of a firearm by a convicted felon
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Middle District of Louisiana
outcome: Pleaded guilty to felon in possession; sentenced to 36 months supervised release (no prison time) — time served (jail), January 2023
source1_description: DOJ MDLA — NBA YoungBoy pleads guilty, sentenced to time served
source2_description: Pitchfork — NBA YoungBoy avoids prison in gun case

name: Lawrence Taylor
role_title: NFL Hall of Fame Linebacker, New York Giants
charges: Sexual misconduct in the second degree — paid $300 for sex with a 16-year-old girl (a trafficking victim)
jurisdiction: Rockland County Court, New York (state)
outcome: Pleaded guilty; sentenced to 6 years probation, required to register as a sex offender
source1_description: AP News — Lawrence Taylor sex offender plea deal
source2_description: ESPN — Lawrence Taylor sentenced to sex offender probation

name: Plaxico Burress
role_title: NFL Wide Receiver, New York Giants (Super Bowl XLII champion)
charges: Criminal possession of a weapon in the second degree — accidentally shot himself with an unlicensed handgun at a Manhattan nightclub
jurisdiction: New York Supreme Court, Manhattan (state)
outcome: 2 years in state prison; released in June 2011 after serving ~20 months
source1_description: New York Daily News — Plaxico Burress sentenced to 2 years
source2_description: ESPN — Plaxico Burress sentenced after nightclub shooting

name: Rae Carruth
role_title: NFL Wide Receiver, Carolina Panthers
charges: Conspiracy to commit murder — hired gunman to shoot his pregnant girlfriend Cherica Adams; she died, their son Chancellor survived with brain damage
jurisdiction: Mecklenburg County Superior Court, North Carolina (state)
outcome: Sentenced to 18–24 years in prison; released October 22, 2018 after serving approximately 18 years
source1_description: ESPN — Rae Carruth released from prison
source2_description: AP News — Rae Carruth murder-for-hire conviction

name: Marion Jones
role_title: Olympic Track and Field Athlete (five medals, 2000 Sydney Olympics)
charges: Two counts of making false statements to federal investigators — lied to investigators about using steroids (THG) and about involvement in a check-fraud scheme
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Southern District of New York
outcome: 6 months in federal prison; sentenced January 11, 2008. Stripped of all five Olympic medals by IOC.
source1_description: DOJ press release — Marion Jones pleads guilty to lying to federal investigators
source2_description: BBC Sport — Marion Jones jailed for six months

name: Oscar Pistorius
role_title: Paralympic and Olympic Sprinter ('Blade Runner'), South Africa
charges: Murder — shot and killed his girlfriend Reeva Steenkamp through a bathroom door on Valentine's Day 2013
jurisdiction: North Gauteng High Court, Pretoria, South Africa
outcome: Originally convicted of culpable homicide (2014), retried and convicted of murder (2015). Sentenced to 13 years and 5 months. Granted parole January 5, 2024 after serving half of sentence.
conviction_date: September 12, 2014 (culpable homicide); December 3, 2015 (murder conviction on appeal)
source1_description: BBC News — Oscar Pistorius murder conviction confirmed
source2_description: Guardian — Oscar Pistorius granted parole after 8 years in prison
